title: Packing Codes for Date and Time Data
breadcrumbs: Packing Codes for Date and Time Data
text: Packing Codes for Date and Time Data Uniface data types Date, Time, and Datetime (and linear variations of these) are associated with a variety of packing codes. The following tables show the default packing codes for each data and time data type, and the complete list of all packing codes for this data. Any of the packing codes may be converted to a more optimum storage format by the DBMS connector interface. For more information, see the relevant connector documentation. Table 1. Default Packing Codes for Date and Time Data Types Uniface data type Default packing code Default layout Date D DIS( ddmmyyyy ) Datetime E DIS( dd-MMM-yyyy hhnnss ) Linear Date D DIS( ddmmyyyy ) Linear Datetime E DIS( dd-MMM-yyyy hhnnss ) Linear Time T DIS( hh:nn:ss ) Time T DIS( hh:nn:ss ) Table 2. Date and Time Packing Codes Uniface Packing Code Explanation Uniface Data Type D LD E LE T LT D Optimum DBMS Date default Y D1 ASCII Date dd-mmm-yyyy Y D2 ASCII Date yyyymmdd Y D3 ASCII Date ddmmyyyy Y D4 ASCII Date yymmdd Y D5 ASCII Date ddmmyy Y D6 Binary Date yymd Y D7 Binary Date dmyy Y D8 Binary Date ymd Y D9 Binary Date dmy Y D10 Binary Date yymmdd Y D11 Binary Date ddmmyy Y D12 ASCII Date mm/dd/yyyy Y D13 ASCII Date yyyy-mm-dd Y E Optimum DBMS combined Datetime default Y Y Y E1 Sybase linear 4-byte Date and 4-byte Time Y Y Y E2 RMS linear Datetime Y Y Y E3 ASCII Date ddmmyyyy hh:nn:ss Y Y Y E4 ASCII Date ddmmyyyy hhnnss Y Y Y E6 ORACLE internal Datetime format Y Y Y E7 Sybase ASCII Date mm/dd/yyyy hh:nn:ss.tt Y Y Y E8 ASCII Datetime yyyymmdd hhnnss (such as D2+T2) Y Y Y E9 Sybase binary Small Datetime Y Y Y E10 ASCII Datetime yyyy-mm-dd hh:nn:ss Y Y Y E11 Oracle date yyyy-mm-dd:hh:nn:ss Y Y Y E12 Oracle timestamp yyyy-mm-dd:hh:nn:ss.t Y Y Y E13 Oracle timestamp yyyy-mm-dd:hh:nn:ss.tt Y Y Y T Optimum DBMS Time default Y T1 ASCII Time hh:nn:ss Y T2 ASCII Time hhnnss Y T3 ASCII ddmmyyyy:hhmmss (mapped to E4) Y T4 Time hh:nn:ss Y T5 Time hh:nn:ss.t Y T6 Time hh:nn:ss.tt Y Z1-Z8 User-defined packing code. (Not for Linear Date, Linear Datetime, or Linear Time.) Y Y Y DBMS Connector Interface Some DBMSs do not have separate Date and Time data types, but combined Datetime data types. When a Time field or a Date field is mapped alone to one of these combined Datetime fields, the missing part is filled with a NULL value; that is, a missing date is filled with whatever is recognized as the NULL date for that DBMS, and a missing time is filled with a NULL time. This is not a problem if Uniface is the only application reading, writing, and manipulating the data. However, if another program can update or manipulate the data, you must ensure that the NULL date is added to the time when storing Time-only values, otherwise Uniface will no longer be able to read the Datetime data. Null Values When data is stored in a DBMS that does not support NULL values, it is not possible to tell the difference between a value of zero and empty (NULL) in date and time fields with packing codes D6 through D11. Record-level DBMSs usually do not support NULL values. If a field with one of these packing codes explicitly receives a value of 0, Uniface displays it as if the field were empty. Display Format and Syntax Definitions Field layout and syntax definitions work separately from the packing code definition. For example, if the field layout defines a field as DIS(dd/MMM/yyyy) , that is how it is always displayed, regardless of the packing code that has been specified. User-Defined Packing Codes You can define your own packing codes for Date, Datetime, and Time fields. To do this, you must define the 3GL format conversion routines required by Uniface.
